tools.name: search_creatives
tools.description: Search mobile game ad creatives by keyword or visual-description-like natural language. For an English request, send the original English concepts only. The service internally expands genre, hook, mood, art-style, and gameplay-mechanic concepts to Chinese source-label equivalents for retrieval; gene

tools.name: get_creative_detail
tools.description: Get one creative's delivery metadata and English creative insights, including opening hook signals, scene descriptions, and creative patterns when available. Example: 'Why does this winning creative work?'

tools.name: get_creative_rankings
tools.description: Get global or genre-level rising creative rankings by day, week, or month, or evergreen creative rankings from the monthly snapshot; platform and country filters are not supported. Evergreen data is monthly only; day/week requests are normalized to month and disclosed in request_echo. Use this to an

tools.name: generate_weekly_creative_brief
tools.description: Generate a compact creative brief from recent high-signal mobile game ads. Use this for weekly inspiration, hook mining, competitor creative planning, and UA brainstorming. Creative tag fields are normalized to English in the result. Example: 'Create this week's puzzle-game creative brief for Facebo

tools.name: search_advertisers
tools.description: Search advertisers, game studios, or mobile games by name and optional genre, region, and platform filters. Use the returned advertiser_id with get_advertiser_profile. Example: 'Find the advertiser for Last War.'

tools.name: get_advertiser_profile
tools.description: Get an advertiser or game profile, including recent ad activity, creative volume, target countries, and platforms. Creative tag fields are normalized to English in the result. Example: 'Which markets and platforms is this game scaling on?'

tools.name: get_reference_image_search_results
tools.description: Get completed reference-image search results. If the job is still running, returns polling guidance. Example: 'Show the matches from my reference-image search.'

tools.name: submit_reference_image_search
tools.description: Submit a reference-image similarity search as an asynchronous job. Use this when the user provides a directly downloadable public HTTPS image URL. The URL must resolve to image bytes without login or hotlink protection; HTML pages, local paths, and base64 are not supported. The embedding provider va

tools.name: find_similar_creatives
tools.description: Find visually similar creatives using the stored vector of an existing creative. For a concept without an ID, query selects an explainable seed from available creative metadata and then uses the same vector-neighbor search. For an English concept, send the original English terms only. The service re

tools.name: get_reference_image_search_status
tools.description: Get the processing status of a reference-image search job. Example: 'Is my reference-image search finished yet?'

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "submit_reference_image_search" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Submit a reference-image similarity search as an asynchronous job. Use this when the user provides a directly downloadable public HTTPS image URL. The URL must …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: submit_reference_image_search · Private data: find_similar_creatives · Public sink: search_creatives, submit_reference_image_search, find_similar_creatives · Destructive: submit_reference_image_search, find_similar_creatives.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: submit_reference_image_search · Private data: find_similar_creatives · Public sink: search_creatives, submit_reference_image_search, find_similar_creatives · Destructive: submit_reference_image_search, find_similar_creatives

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
